title: osteomeles cordata
intro: Osteomeles cordata, also known as the Hawaiian hawthorn or mamala hoawa, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae that is endemic to the Hawaiian Islands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation due to its anthraquinone and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Osteomeles cordata include its potential to soothe gastrointestinal issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
